root_text.an9.82:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an9.82:2.1: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya cattāro sammappadhānā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an9.82:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an9.82:2.6: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.an9.82:2.7: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya ime cattāro sammappadhānā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an9.82:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.82:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant isn’t free of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for sensual pleasures. …
translation_text.an9.82:1.4: These are the five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.82:2.1: To give up these five shackles of the heart you should develop the four right efforts.
translation_text.an9.82:2.3: It’s when a mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise. …
translation_text.an9.82:2.4: so that unskillful qualities are given up …
translation_text.an9.82:2.6: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are completed by development.
translation_text.an9.82:2.7: To give up these five shackles of the hearts you should develop these four right efforts.”
